# US Stock Watcher Next-Session Forecast — Friday 7/17

> **[Friday 7/17 session outlook] bullish lean — Moderate / selective risk-on · composite +28 · confidence 44%**

> **🔮 Next-session forecast** — The next U.S. regular session is Friday 7/17 (opens 9:30 AM ET / 22:30 KST 7/17).

_Data as of: 2026-07-17T05:58:12 · data quality: fresh_

## Executive Summary

With markets currently closed, this brief looks ahead to the next U.S. regular session, Friday 7/17, which opens at 9:30 AM ET (22:30 KST on 7/17). Heading into that session, the overall market-state score stands at +28 out of 100, placing conditions in a "Moderate / selective risk-on" posture, with confidence in that read at 44% based on four measured components. Several inputs remain unmeasured for this cycle — liquidity, credit, earnings, macro surprises, valuation, and positioning were all reweighted out of the composite — so the signal should be treated as directionally useful but partial.

## Market Diagnosis

**Indices.** Heading into Friday's open, the major benchmarks carry the following most recent readings: the S&P 500 sits at 7,533.77, down 0.51% on the day; the Nasdaq-100 is at 29,025.77, off 1.62%; the Dow stands at 52,552.97, down 0.20%; and the Russell 2000 is at 2,974.57, down a more modest 0.06%.

**Market drivers.** The composite backdrop is being shaped by a mixed set of forces: volatility is currently supportive of risk assets, while index trend is acting as a headwind. On the rates side, the yield curve is supportive, whereas the US dollar is working against the risk-on case as a headwind.

**Breadth & concentration.** The overall market-state score of +28/100 continues to anchor the "Moderate / selective risk-on" characterization, with confidence held at 44% across the four components currently measured. As noted above, liquidity, credit, earnings, macro surprises, valuation, and positioning remain unmeasured and have been reweighted out of this calculation, so breadth conviction should be read with that caveat in mind.

**Sector & rotation.** Leadership into the next session is concentrated in Financials, Health Care, and Energy, while Consumer Discretionary, Materials, and Technology are lagging — a rotation pattern that skews toward defensive and value-oriented cyclicals over growth-sensitive groups.

**Rates, dollar & commodities.** The 10-year Treasury yield is at 4.55%. The dollar index (DXY) is at 100.75, up a slight 0.02% on the day. In commodities, WTI crude is at $78.30, down 0.82%, while gold is at $3,990.30, up 0.12%.

## Outlook

**Short term.** Framed specifically for Friday 7/17's session (opening 9:30 AM ET / 22:30 KST), the near-term bias is constructive, consistent with the "Moderate / selective risk-on" market state and its 44% confidence level. That said, this lean is conditional: the view flips should the overall composite score cross below zero.

**Medium term.** The medium-term outlook carries the same constructive bias, again grounded in the "Moderate / selective risk-on" designation at 44% confidence — with the identical caveat that a move of the overall score across the zero line would invalidate this stance.

**Medium-to-long term.** Extending further out, the medium-to-long-term view likewise leans constructive under the current "Moderate / selective risk-on" read (44% confidence), subject to the same trigger: a crossing of the composite score below zero would flip the outlook.

## Top News

Coverage feeding into this brief includes KraneShares asking whether investors are overlooking a key component in the global AI supply chain via its China Semiconductor ETF commentary; Moomoo reporting that Wall Street remains firmly bullish on Taiwan Semiconductor's aggressive investment plans, with AI demand visibility extending out to 2030 and offering a tailwind for semiconductor equipment stocks; NAI500 noting that Federal Reserve officials have intensified their public communications in a way that is shaking easing expectations; FXStreet's morning briefing observing that US Treasury yields remain lower and stable; marketscreener.com noting that the S&P 500 index slipped slightly after the Stockholm stock market closed on Thursday; and simplywall.st highlighting dividend stocks to watch as lower Treasury yields lift defensive utilities.
